scope.summary: Implement a reset_to_rest() API call on the ClothSolver trait that rewinds all particle positions and velocities to the initial panel arrangement (as computed by MT-119) without clearing the loaded garment, seams, or material params, enabling parameter-adjust-then-re-sim cycles without full reload.
acceptance_criteria: ClothSolver trait gains fn reset_to_rest(&mut self) -> Result<(), SolverError> that restores particle positions to the initial arrangement snapshot taken after MT-119 placement.
Calling reset_to_rest() followed by simulate() produces the same result as a fresh load + simulate() within MeshComparator eps_mm tolerance.
The animation API (MT-294) exposes a reset_simulation endpoint for model and operator use.
reset_to_rest() emits TailorSimRunStarted with a reset=true flag in metadata so the EventLedger trail is unambiguous.
scope.risk_if_missed: Every parameter adjustment requires a full garment reload through the GarmentSpec -> tessellate -> ingest pipeline (MT-004, MT-127), making iterative sim tuning expensive. The typical MD creative loop of adjust-fabric -> reset -> resim is not available as a lightweight call, significantly slowing model-driven parameter search.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
